description: Embedded core list for Stage 1 word filter. RECALL-ONLY — Stage 2 LLM disambiguates context (use/mention, AAVE reclamation, quotation, song lyrics, educational framing).
curation_note: This seed is the minimum viable hard-tier set. It is intentionally conservative. Admins should extend via the DB-backed moderation_terms table (source='admin') based on observed patterns. Entries here cannot be deleted, only disabled. To add or remove from this file, update both the JSON and the SHA-256 checksum sibling file — the server fails closed on a mismatch.
stage_role: Stage 1 only flags; never auto-bans. A flag escalates to Stage 2 (Groq LLM with AAVE/quotation guidance). The hard-tier flag does NOT mean automatic action.

terms.notes: Hard-r form. Stage 2 must disambiguate AAVE reclamation context (the soft-a 'nigga' form is intentionally NOT in the embedded hard tier per the AAVE-bias accepted risk discussion).
Anti-Jewish slur.
Anti-East-Asian slur. Note: 'chink in the armor' idiom exists; Stage 2 must use context.
Anti-East-Asian slur, originating from Korean War era.
Anti-Mexican/Latino slur.
Anti-Arab/Sikh/Muslim slur, often targeting head-covering practices.
Homophobic slur. The 'fag' alone is intentionally excluded due to too many false-positive uses (British English 'cigarette', surname, etc.).
Transphobic slur. Note: 'tranny' also slang for automotive transmission; Stage 2 must disambiguate.
Anti-Black racial slur.
Anti-Latino slur. Note: also a common surname in some communities; admin should review false positives.
Soft-a form, widely reclaimed in AAVE. Soft tier — flag for Stage 2 evaluation only; never the basis of auto-action without strong out-of-AAVE context evidence.
Reclaimed within the lesbian community. Soft tier for Stage 2 context evaluation.
Reclaimed within the LGBTQ+ community. Soft tier.
Ableist; major platforms have moved away from auto-banning. Soft tier.
Anti-Black slur derived from folk story; sometimes used metaphorically. Soft tier.
Explicit incitement to genocide. Stage 2 will not find a defensible use case.
Explicit Holocaust reference as incitement.
School-shooting threat phrase.
Mass-violence threat phrase pattern; Stage 2 evaluates target.
Suicide-bait phrase, commonly hurled at targets in harassment campaigns.
Internet shorthand for 'kill yourself'. Soft tier because Stage 2 must consider context (anger expressed at oneself vs. directed at another).
White-nationalist dog whistle. Soft tier — Stage 2 evaluates surrounding rhetoric.
White-nationalist slogan reference. Soft tier.
Anti-Muslim ethnic-cleansing meme phrase. Soft tier — meme deployment varies.
CSAM reference.
CSAM reference.
CSAM-adjacent.
CSAM-adjacent phrase. Stage 2 considers context (e.g., news report citation vs. solicitation).
CSAM-adjacent phrase.
Sexualization of minors.
Anime fandom term that can be benign (Lolita-style character archetype) or CSAM-coded. Soft tier.
Anime fandom term with similar issues; soft tier.
